statement: If a linear map satisfies `f ∘ f = 0`, then `range f ⊆ ker f`; in finite dimension, its rank is at most half the dimension.
lean_statement:
theorem range_le_ker_and_finrank_le_half_of_comp_self_eq_zero
    {K V : Type*} [Field K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V] [FiniteDimensional K V]
    (f : Module.End K V) (hf : f * f = 0) :
    LinearMap.range f ≤ LinearMap.ker f ∧
      Module.finrank K (LinearMap.range f) ≤ Module.finrank K V / 2
proof:
Proof: if `y ∈ range f`, then `y = f x` for some `x`, so `f y = f (f x) = 0`; hence `y ∈ ker f`.
Therefore `range f ≤ ker f`.

If `V` is finite-dimensional, rank-nullity gives
`finrank(range f) + finrank(ker f) = finrank(V)`. Since `range f ≤ ker f`, we have
`finrank(range f) ≤ finrank(ker f)`, so
`2 * finrank(range f) ≤ finrank(V)`, i.e. `finrank(range f) ≤ finrank(V) / 2`.
